mechanism_narrative: Sustained near-zero federal funds rate (0.09%) keeps the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows compressed, supporting continued valuation expansion in the S&P 500 over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of t-1mo, unchanged near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05) while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of UNRATE below the 6.0% psychological threshold, confirmed by continued PAYEMS gains, signals a structurally tightening labor market that boosts consumer income expectations and corporate earnings outlooks, driving S&P 500 gains over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% on a monthly reading
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.2% as of t-1mo, trending down from cycle peak
regime_hint: labor market tightening phase of expansion
failure_condition: In the 6 months following UNRATE first crossing below 6.0%, 60d forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion that is not captured by either series alone, and this joint confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as it reduces recession risk pricing.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.39 and RSAFS at 439802 million as of latest snapshot, both trending upward
failure_condition: When both triggers are active jointly, 20d forward SPX returns fail to exceed the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin (t-test p>0.05) across at least 2 independent trigger episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) strength is amplified specifically when federal funds rate remains near zero, because cheap mortgage financing converts housing momentum into broader wealth effects and construction-linked equity earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this effect is muted or reversed outside a ZIRP regime.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 893k units and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: During episodes where FEDFUNDS rises above 1% while HOUST continues rising, the positive HOUST-return relationship persists unchanged (no regime dependence detected), or 60d returns show no significant difference between ZIRP and non-ZIRP subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in headline CPI year-over-year inflation above 2.5% raises market expectations of an earlier-than-expected end to accommodative policy, triggering short-term equity risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.693 as of t-2mo, consistent with sub-2.5% yoy inflation
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL yoy exceeds 2.5%, 5d forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects an ongoing expansionary business cycle that compresses the equity risk premium and supports S&P 500 appreciation over the subsequent quarter, independent of any threshold or trigger condition.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15985.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of t-4mo, positive growth trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth trend and 60d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns during GDP expansion quarters are not significantly higher than during contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Joint confirmation of robust monthly payroll gains (PAYEMS) with a flat or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals genuine labor market strength rather than a statistical artifact of labor force exits, and this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; divergence between the two series (e.g., payroll gains with rising unemployment) invalidates the signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month gain exceeds 150k AND UNRATE is flat or declining
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139004 thousand and UNRATE at 6.2% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly met, 20d forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over a rolling 3-year sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of negative industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals an emerging supply-side slowdown that triggers a growth scare among equity investors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth is negative for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.3882 as of t-1mo, currently on an upward trajectory
failure_condition: Following two consecutive months of negative INDPRO growth, 5d forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or such episodes show no meaningful divergence from unconditional 5d return distribution.
